Strange Ball: An unusual Ball warped through space and time.
Poké Ball: A tool used for catching wild Pokémon.
Great Ball: A good Ball with a higher catch rate than a Poké Ball.
Ultra Ball: A better Ball with a higher catch rate than a Great Ball.
Master Ball: The best Ball that catches a Pokémon without fail.
Premier Ball: A rare Ball made in commemoration of some event.
Heal Ball: A remedial Ball that restores caught Pokémon.
Net Ball: A Ball that works well on Water- and Bug-type Pokémon.
Nest Ball: A Ball that works better on weaker Pokémon.
Dive Ball: A Ball that works better on Pokémon on the ocean floor.
Dusk Ball: Works well if used in a dark place.
Timer Ball: A Ball that gains power in battles taking many turns.
Quick Ball: Works well if used on the first turn.
Repeat Ball: A Ball that works better on Pokémon caught before.
Luxury Ball: A cozy Ball that makes Pokémon more friendly.
Level Ball: A Ball that works well on lower level Pokémon.
Lure Ball: A Ball that works well on fished up Pokémon.
Moon Ball: A Ball that works well on Moon Stone users.
Friend Ball: A Ball that makes a Pokémon friendly when caught.
Love Ball: Works well on Pokémon of the opposite gender.
Fast Ball: Works well on very fast Pokémon.
Heavy Ball: Works well on very heavy Pokémon.
Dream Ball: A Ball that works well on sleeping Pokémon.
Safari Ball: A special Ball that is used only in the Safari Zone.
Sport Ball: A special Ball used in the Bug- Catching Contest.
Park Ball: A special Ball for the Pal Park.
Beast Ball: A Ball designed to catch Ultra Beasts.
Cherish Ball: A rare Ball made in commemoration of some event.
Potion: Restores the HP of a Pokémon by 20 points.
Super Potion: Restores the HP of a Pokémon by 60 points.
Hyper Potion: Restores the HP of a Pokémon by 120 points.
Max Potion: Fully restores the HP of a Pokémon.
Full Restore: Fully restores the HP and status of a Pokémon.
Revive: Revives a fainted Pokémon with half its HP.
Max Revive: Revives a fainted Pokémon with all its HP.
Fresh Water: A mineral water that restores HP by 30 points.
Soda Pop: A fizzy soda drink that restores HP by 50 points.
Lemonade: A very sweet drink that restores HP by 80 points.
Moomoo Milk: A nutritious milk that restores HP by 100 points.
Energy Powder: A bitter powder that restores HP by 60 points.
Energy Root: A bitter root that restores HP by 120 points.
Heal Powder: A bitter powder that heals all status problems.
Revival Herb: A very bitter herb that revives a fainted Pokémon.
Full Heal: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Ether: Restores the PP of a selected move by 10.
Max Ether: Fully restores the PP of a selected move.
Elixir: Restores the PP of all moves by 10.
Max Elixir: Fully restores the PP of a Pokémon's moves.
Berry Juice: A 100% pure juice that restores HP by 20 points.
Sacred Ash: Fully revives and restores all fainted Pokémon.
Sweet Heart: A sweet chocolate that restores HP by 20 points.
Max Honey: Revives a fainted Pokémon with all its HP.
Pewter Crunchies: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Rage Candy Bar: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Lava Cookie: A local specialty that heals all status problems.

Casteliacone: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Lumiose Galette: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Shalour Sable: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Big Malasada: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.

Prevents stat reduction when used in battle.

Icy Rock: Extends the length of cold weathers used by the holder.
Electric Seed: Boosts Defense on Electric Terrain, but only one time.
Psychic Seed: Boosts Sp. Def. on Psychic Terrain, but only one time.
Misty Seed: Boosts Sp. Def. on Misty Terrain, but only one time.
Grassy Seed: Boosts Defense on Grassy Terrain, but only one time.
Absorb Bulb: Raises Sp. Atk if the holder is hit by a Water-type move.
Cell Battery: Raises Attack if the holder is hit by an Electric move.
Luminous Moss: Raises Sp. Def if the holder is hit by a Water-type move.
Snowball: Raises Atk if its holder is hit by an Ice-type move.
Bright Powder: A hold item that casts a glare to reduce accuracy.
White Herb: A hold item that restores any lowered stat.
Exp. Share: A hold item that gets Exp. points from battles.
Quick Claw: A hold item that occasionally allows the first strike.
Soothe Bell: A hold item that calms spirits and fosters friendship.
Mental Herb: Snaps Pokémon out of move-binding effects.
King's Rock: A hold item that may cause flinching when the foe is hit.
Amulet Coin: Doubles money in battle if the holder takes part.
Cleanse Tag: A hold item that helps repel wild Pokémon.
Smoke Ball: A hold item that assures fleeing from wild Pokémon.
Focus Band: A hold item that occasionally prevents fainting.
Lucky Egg: A hold item that boosts Exp. points earned in battle.
Scope Lens: A hold item that improves the critical-hit rate.
Leftovers: A hold item that gradually restores HP in battle.
Shell Bell: A hold item that restores HP upon striking the foe.
Wide Lens: A magnifying lens that boosts the accuracy of moves.
Muscle Band: A headband that boosts the power of physical moves.
Wise Glasses: A pair of glasses that ups the power of special moves.
Expert Belt: A belt that boosts the power of super effective moves.
Light Clay: Extends the length of barrier moves used by the holder.
Life Orb: Boosts move power but holder loses HP with each attack.
Power Herb: Allows immediate use of a move that charges first.
Focus Sash: If the holder has full HP, it endures KO hits with 1 HP.
Zoom Lens: If the holder moves after the foe, it'll boost accuracy.
Metronome: A held item that boosts a move used consecutively.
Iron Ball: Cuts Speed and becomes vulnerable to Ground moves.
Lagging Tail: A held item that makes the holder move slower.
Destiny Knot: If the holder falls in love, the foe does too.
Black Sludge: Restores HP for Poison-types. Damages all others.
Grip Claw: A held item that extends binding moves like Wrap.
Sticky Barb: Damages the holder each turn. May latch on to foes.
Shed Shell: Allows the holder to switch out without fail.
Big Root: A held item that ups the power of HP-stealing moves.
Razor Claw: A hooked claw that ups the holder's critical-hit ratio.
Razor Fang: A hold item that may cause flinching when the foe is hit.
Eviolite: Raises the Def and Sp. Def of Pokémon that can evolve.
Float Stone: It's so light that when held, it halves a Pokémon's weight.
Rocky Helmet: Hurts the foe if they touch its holder.
Air Balloon: Makes the holder float but bursts if hit by an attack.
Red Card: Switches out the foe if they hit the holder.
Ring Target: Moves that usually have no effect will hit the holder.
Binding Band: Increases the power of binding moves when held.
Eject Button: Switches out the user if they're hit by the foe.
Weakness Policy: If hit by a super- effective move, ups Atk and Sp. Atk.
Assault Vest: Raises Sp. Def but prevents the use of status moves.
Safety Goggles: Protect from weather damage and powder moves.
Adrenaline Orb: This orb boosts Speed if the holder is intimidated.
Terrain Extender: Extends the length of the active battle terrain.
Protective Pads: Guard the holder from contact move effects.
Throat Spray: Raises Sp. Atk. if the holder uses a sound-based move.
Eject Pack: Forces the user to switch if its stats are lowered.
Heavy-Duty Boots: Boots that prevent effects of traps set in the field.
Blunder Policy: Raises Speed if the user misses due to Accuracy.
Room Service: Lowers Speed if Trick Room is active.
Utility Umbrella: An umbrella that protects from weather effects.
Cheri Berry: A hold item that heals paralysis in battle.
Chesto Berry: A hold item that awakens Pokémon in battle.
Pecha Berry: A hold item that heals poisoning in battle.
Rawst Berry: A hold item that heals a burn in battle.
Aspear Berry: A hold item that defrosts Pokémon in battle.
Leppa Berry: A hold item that restores 10 PP in battle.
Oran Berry: A hold item that restores 10 HP in battle.
Persim Berry: A hold item that heals confusion in battle.
Lum Berry: A hold item that heals any status problem in battle.
Sitrus Berry: A hold item that restores 30 HP in battle.
Figy Berry: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
Wiki Berry: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
Mago Berry: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
Aguav Berry: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
Iapapa Berry: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
Razz Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Razz.
Bluk Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Bluk.
Nanab Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Nanab.
Wepear Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Wepear.
Pinap Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Pinap.
Pomeg Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base HP.
Kelpsy Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base Attack.
Qualot Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base Defense.
Hondew Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base Sp. Atk.
Grepa Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base Sp. Def.
Tamato Berry: Makes a Pokémon friendly but lowers base Speed.
Cornn Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Cornn.
Magost Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Magost.
Rabuta Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Rabuta.
Nomel Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Nomel.
Spelon Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Spelon.
Pamtre Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Pamtre.
Watmel Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Watmel.
Durin Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Durin.
Belue Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Belue.
Chilan Berry: A hold item that weakens a Normal move.
Occa Berry: A hold item that weakens a Fire move if weak to it.
Passho Berry: A hold item that weakens a Water move if weak to it.
Wacan Berry: A hold item that weakens a Electric move if weak to it.
Rindo Berry: A hold item that weakens a Grass move if weak to it.
Yache Berry: A hold item that weakens a Ice move if weak to it.
Chople Berry: A hold item that weakens a Fighting move if weak to it.
Kebia Berry: A hold item that weakens a Poison move if weak to it.
Shuca Berry: A hold item that weakens a Ground move if weak to it.
Coba Berry: A hold item that weakens a Flying move if weak to it.
Payapa Berry: A hold item that weakens a Psychic move if weak to it.
Tanga Berry: A hold item that weakens a Bug move if weak to it.
Charti Berry: A hold item that weakens a Rock move if weak to it.
Kasib Berry: A hold item that weakens a Ghost move if weak to it.
Haban Berry: A hold item that weakens a Dragon move if weak to it.
Colbur Berry: A hold item that weakens a Dark move if weak to it.
Babiri Berry: A hold item that weakens a Steel move if weak to it.
Roseli Berry: A hold item that weakens a Fairy move if weak to it.
Liechi Berry: A hold item that raises Attack in a pinch.
Ganlon Berry: A hold item that raises Defense in a pinch.
Salac Berry: A hold item that raises Speed in a pinch.
Petaya Berry: A hold item that raises Sp. Atk in a pinch.
Apicot Berry: A hold item that raises Sp. Def in a pinch.
Lansat Berry: A hold item that ups the critical- hit rate in a pinch.
Starf Berry: A hold item that sharply boosts a stat in a pinch.
Enigma Berry: {POKEBLOCK} ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow a mystery.
Micle Berry: When held, it ups the Accuracy of a move in a pinch.
Custap Berry: It allows a Pokémon in a pinch to move first just once.
Jaboca Berry: If hit by a physical move, it will hurt the attacker a bit.
Rowap Berry: If hit by a special move, it will hurt the attacker a bit.
Kee Berry: If hit by a physical move, it raises the Defense a bit.
Maranga Berry: If hit by a special move, it raises the Sp. Def. a bit.
TM01: Powerful, but makes the user flinch if hit by the foe.
TM02: Hooks and slashes the foe with long, sharp claws.
TM03: Generates an ultrasonic wave that may confuse.
TM04: Raises Sp. Atk and Sp. Def by focusing the mind.
TM06: Poisons the foe with a toxin that gradually worsens.
TM07: Creates a hailstorm that damages all types except Ice.
TM08: Bulks up the body to boost both Attack & Defense.
TM09: Shoots 2 to 5 seeds in a row to strike the foe.
TM10: The attack power varies among different Pokémon.
TM11: Raises the power of Fire-type moves for 5 turns.
TM12: Enrages the foe so it can only use attack moves.
TM13: Fires an icy cold beam that may inflict frostbite.
TM14: A snow-and-wind attack that may inflict frostbite.
TM15: Powerful, but needs recharging the next turn.
TM16: Creates a wall of light that lowers Sp. Atk damage.
TM17: Negates all damage, but may fail if used in succession.
TM18: Raises the power of Water-type moves for 5 turns.
TM19: Recovers half the HP of the damage this move inflicts.
TM20: Prevents status abnormality with a mystical power.
TM21: The less the user likes you, the more powerful this move.
TM22: Absorbs sunlight in the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.
TM23: Slams the foe with a hard tail. It may lower Defense.
TM24: A powerful electric attack that may cause paralysis.
TM25: Strikes the foe with a thunderbolt. It may paralyze.
TM26: Causes a quake that has no effect on flying foes.
TM27: The more the user likes you, the more powerful this move.
TM28: Digs underground the 1st turn, then strikes next turn.
TM29: A powerful psychic attack that may lower Sp. Def.
TM30: Hurls a dark lump at the foe. It may lower Sp. Def.
TM31: Destroys barriers like Light Screen and causes damage.
TM32: Creates illusory copies to enhance elusiveness.
TM33: Creates a wall of light that weakens physical attacks.
TM34: Zaps the foe with a jolt of electricity that never misses.
TM35: Looses a stream of fire that may burn the foe.
TM36: Hurls sludge at the foe. It may poison the foe.
TM37: Causes a sandstorm that hits the foe over several turns.
TM38: A powerful fire attack that may burn the foe.
TM39: Stops the foe from moving with rocks. May lower Speed.
TM40: An extremely fast attack that can't be avoided.
TM41: Prevents the foe from using the same move in a row.
TM42: Raises Attack when poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.
TM43: Adds an effect to attack depending on the location.
TM44: The user sleeps for 2 turns to restore health and status.
TM45: Makes it tough to attack a foe of the opposite gender.
TM46: While attacking, it may steal the foe's held item.
TM47: Spreads hard- edged wings and slams into the foe.
TM48: Switches abilities with the foe on the turn this is used.
TM49: Steals the effects of the move the foe is trying to use.
TM50: Enables full-power attack, but sharply lowers Sp. Atk.
HM01: Attacks the foe with sharp blades or claws.
HM02: Flies up on the first turn, then attacks next turn.
HM03: Creates a huge wave, then crashes it down on the foe.
HM04: Builds enormous power, then slams the foe.
HM05: Looses a powerful blast of light that reduces accuracy.
HM06: A rock-crushingly tough attack that may lower Defense.
HM07: Attacks the foe with enough power to climb waterfalls.
HM08: Dives underwater the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.
Oval Charm: Raises the chance of finding eggs at the daycare.
Shiny Charm: A charm that will raise the chance of Shiny Pokémon.
Catching Charm: A charm that raises the chance of Critical Captures.
Exp. Charm: A charm that raises the amount of Exp. earned in battle.
Rotom Catalog: A catalog full of devices liked by Rotom.
Gracidea: Bouquets made with it are offered as a token of gratitude.
Reveal Glass: This glass returns a Pokémon back to its original form.
DNA Splicers: Splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon.
Zygarde Cube: An item to store Zygarde Cores and Cells.
Prison Bottle: A bottle used to seal a certain Pokémon long ago.
N-Solarizer: A device to fuse and split Necrozma using a Solgaleo.
N-Lunarizer: A device to fuse and split Necrozma using a Lunala.
Reins of Unity: Reins that unite Calyrex with its beloved steed.
Mega Ring: Enables {PKMN} holding their Mega Stone to Mega Evolve.
Z-Power Ring: A strange ring that enables Z-Move usage.
Dynamax Band: A band carrying a Wishing Star that allows Dynamaxing.
Bicycle: A folding bicycle that is faster than the Running Shoes.
Mach Bike: A folding bicycle that doubles your speed or better.
Acro Bike: A folding bicycle capable of jumps and wheelies.
Old Rod: Use by any body of water to fish for wild Pokémon.
Good Rod: A decent fishing rod for catching wild Pokémon.
Super Rod: The best fishing rod for catching wild Pokémon.
Dowsing Machine: A device that signals an invisible item by sound.
Town Map: Can be viewed anytime. Shows your present location.
Vs. Seeker: A rechargeable unit that flags battle- ready Trainers.
TM Case: A convenient case that holds TMs and HMs.
Berry Pouch: A convenient container that holds Berries.
{PKMN} Box Link: This device grants access to the {PKMN} Storage System.
Coin Case: A case that holds up to 9,999 Coins.
Powder Jar: Stores Berry Powder made using a Berry Crusher.
Wailmer Pail: A tool used for watering Berries and plants.
Poké Radar: A tool used to search out Pokémon hiding in grass.
{POKEBLOCK} Case: A case for holding {POKEBLOCK}s made with a Berry Blender.
Soot Sack: A sack used to gather and hold volcanic ash.
Poké Flute: A sweet-sounding flute that awakens Pokémon.
Fame Checker: Stores information on famous people for instant recall.
Teachy TV: A TV set tuned to an advice program for Trainers.
S.S. Ticket: The ticket required for sailing on a ferry.
Eon Ticket: The ticket for a ferry to a distant southern island.
Mystic Ticket: A ticket required to board the ship to Navel Rock.
Aurora Ticket: A ticket required to board the ship to Birth Island.
Old Sea Map: A faded sea chart that shows the way to a certain island.
Letter: A letter to Steven from the President of the Devon Corp.
Devon Parts: A package that contains Devon's machine parts.
Go-Goggles: Nifty goggles that protect eyes from desert sandstorms.
Devon Scope: A device by Devon that signals any unseeable Pokémon.
Basement Key: The key for New Mauville beneath Mauville City.
Scanner: A device found inside the Abandoned Ship.
Storage Key: The key to the storage inside the Abandoned Ship.
Key to Room 1: A key that opens a door inside the Abandoned Ship.
Key to Room 2: A key that opens a door inside the Abandoned Ship.
Key to Room 4: A key that opens a door inside the Abandoned Ship.
Key to Room 6: A key that opens a door inside the Abandoned Ship.
Meteorite: A meteorite found at Meteor Falls.
Magma Emblem: A medal-like item in the same shape as Team Magma's mark.
Contest Pass: The pass required for entering Pokémon Contests.
Parcel: A parcel for Prof. Oak from a Pokémon Mart's clerk.
Secret Key: The key to the Cinnabar Island Gym's entrance.
Bike Voucher: A voucher for obtaining a bicycle from the Bike Shop.
Gold Teeth: Gold dentures lost by the Safari Zone's Warden.
Card Key: A card-type door key used in Silph Co's office.
Lift Key: An elevator key used in Team Rocket's Hideout.
Silph Scope: Silph Co's scope makes unseeable POKéMON visible.
Tri-Pass: A pass for ferries between One, Two, and Three Island.
Rainbow Pass: For ferries serving Vermilion and the Sevii Islands.
Tea: A thirst-quenching tea prepared by an old lady.
Ruby: An exquisite, red- glowing gem that symbolizes passion.
Sapphire: A brilliant blue gem that symbolizes honesty.
Ability Shield: Ability changes are prevented for this items's holder.
Clear Amulet: Stat lowering is prevented for this items's holder.
Punching Glove: Powers up punching moves and removes their contact.
Covert Cloak: Protects holder from additional effects of moves.
Loaded Dice: Rolls high numbers. Multihit strikes hit more times.
Auspicious Armor: Armor inhabited by auspicious wishes. Causes evolution.
Booster Energy: Encapsuled energy ups Pokémon with certain Abilities.
Big Bamboo Shoot: A large and rare bamboo shoot. Best sold to gourmands.
Gimmighoul Coin: Gimmighoul hoard and treasure these curious coins.
Leader's Crest: A shard of an old blade of some sort. Held by Bisharp.
Malicious Armor: Armor inhabited by malicious will. Causes evolution.
Mirror Herb: Mirrors an enemy's stat increases but only once.
Scroll of Darkness: A peculiar scroll with secrets of the dark path.
Scroll of Waters: A peculiar scroll with secrets of the water path.
Tera Orb: Energy charges can be used to cause Terastallization.
Tiny Bamboo Shoot: A small and rare bamboo shoot. Best sold to gourmands.
Bug Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Dark Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Dragon Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.

Flying Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Ghost Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Grass Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Ground Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Ice Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Normal Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Poison Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Psychic Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Rock Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Steel Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Water Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Adamant Crystal: A large, glowing gem that lets Dialga change form.
Griseous Core: A large, glowing gem that lets Giratina change form.
Lustrous Globe: A large, glowing gem that lets Palkia change form.
Black Augurite: A black stone that makes some Pokémon evolve.
Linking Cord: A mysterious string that makes some Pokémon evolve.
Peat Block: A block of material that makes some Pokémon evolve.
Berserk Gene: Sharply boosts Attack, but causes lasting confusion.
Fairy Feather: A hold item that raises the power of Fairy-type moves.
Syrupy Apple: A very syrupy apple that makes certain Pokémon evolve.
Unremarkable Teacup: A cracked teacup that makes certain Pokémon evolve.
Masterpiece Teacup: A chipped teacup that makes certain Pokémon evolve.
Cornerstone Mask: Allows Ogerpon to wield the Rock- type in battle.
Wellspring Mask: Allows Ogerpon to wield the Water- type in battle.
Hearthflame Mask: Allows Ogerpon to wield the Fire- type in battle.
Health Mochi: An item that raises the base HP of a Pokémon.
Muscle Mochi: An item that raises the base Attack of a Pokémon.
Resist Mochi: An item that raises the base Defense of a Pokémon.
Genius Mochi: An item that raises the base Sp. Atk. of a Pokémon.
Clever Mochi: An item that raises the base Sp. Def. of a Pokémon.
Swift Mochi: An item that raises the base Speed of a Pokémon.
Fresh Start Mochi: An item that resets all base points of a Pokémon.
Glimmering Charm: A charm that will raise the shards from Tera Raids.
Metal Alloy: A peculiar metal that makes certain Pokémon evolve.
Stellar Tera Shard: These shards may form when a Tera Pokémon faints.
Jubilife Muffin: Heals all the status problems of one Pokémon.
Remedy: A bitter powder that restores HP by 20 points.
Fine Remedy: A bitter powder that restores HP by 60 points.
Superb Remedy: A bitter powder that restores HP by 120 points.
Aux Evasion: Sharply raises evasiveness during one battle.
Aux Guard: Sharply raises defenses during one battle.
Aux Power: Sharply raises offenses during one battle.
Aux Powerguard: Sharply raises offense & defense during one battle.
Pokéshi Doll: A wooden toy resembling a Poké-. mon. Can be sold.
